claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my daughter Alex - she's 4 years old and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have a 4-year-old daughter named Alex who lives at home with you.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions:

**Where do you currently live? Are you residing in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And just to confirm: **Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for her daily needs such as food, clothes, and general upkeep?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for her care - I provide for all her daily needs like food, clothes, and everything else she needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation provided by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, she was in care for a short period recently - just a couple of months - but she's back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I need to understand this situation a bit better as it may affect eligibility.

**How long exactly was Alex in care (how many weeks), and is she now living with you full-time again? Also, while she was in care, was the local council paying towards her accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It was exactly 4 weeks that she was in care. Yes, she's living with me full-time now. And no, the council wasn't paying towards her accommodation or maintenance while she was there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex was in care for only 4 weeks (which is less than the 8-week threshold), and she's now back home with you full-time, that shouldn't affect your eligibility.

Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or were you born in the UK/are you a UK citizen, or do you have any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen, so there's no issue with my immigration status.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 4 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement
✓ You live in the UK and are a UK citizen
✓ Alex lives with you and you're responsible for her care, providing for all her daily needs
✓ Although Alex was in care for 4 weeks, this is below the 8-week threshold that would typically affect entitlement, and the council wasn't paying for her care
✓ Alex is now back home with you full-time

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for your daughter.

**Please note:** Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so make sure no one else is already claiming for Alex. If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit based on the following: (1) She is under 16 years old (currently 4 years old), which meets the age requirement. (2) She lives with the claimant in the UK, establishing responsibility for her care. (3) The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and provides for all her daily needs including food, clothes, and general upkeep. (4) The claimant is a UK citizen and lives in the UK, meeting the residence requirements. (5) Although Alex was in care for 4 weeks, this is below the 8-week threshold that would affect Child Benefit entitlement, and the local council was not paying towards her accommodation or maintenance during that time. (6) Alex is now living full-time with the claimant again. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with responsibility for their 4-year-old daughter who lives with them. Although Alex was briefly in care for 4 weeks, this is below the 8-week threshold and the council did not pay towards her care. She is now back home full-time. All eligibility requirements are met.
